summary: End-to-end testing of AlphaLens MVP (backend APIs + SSE multi-agent pipeline + React dashboard). Backend: 16/16 pytest tests passing including a real SSE run (AAPL completed in ~55-90s, verdict=HOLD, confidence=62). Frontend: landing, search dropdown, suggestion pills, navigation, dashboard pipeline strip with all 7 agent statuses, verdict-badge and all dashboard cards rendered correctly. SSE streaming works end-to-end through the public ingress.

minor.issue: Searching by exact ticker symbol returns only TSLA-related ETFs, not the underlying TSLA stock. Users typing the ticker itself (a very common UX) won't find AAPL/TSLA/NVDA at the top. Searching by company name works fine. Consider prepending an exact-ticker quote/profile lookup to the results.
Intermittent 10s ingress timeout observed once on a hot run. Non-deterministic, not reproducible on retry.

issues: No data-testid on the confidence gauge element (spec called for 'gauge'). The gauge visually renders inside the verdict block, but a data-testid='gauge' attribute is missing.

action_items: Improve /api/search: when query matches an exact ticker (uppercased, <=5 chars), include the actual underlying security at the top of results (currently TSLA -> only ETFs).
Add data-testid='gauge' to the confidence gauge component in Dashboard.jsx for testability (spec requirement).
(Optional) Add a data-testid='confidence-score' near the numeric value for assertions.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: /api/research/start streams responses but persists the report only after the generator finishes. If a client disconnects mid-stream, the report will still be saved (good) — verified by reading code. Consider catching CancelledError to log disconnects.
Dashboard.jsx is 571 lines — approaching the 700 LOC threshold; consider extracting the agent strip, verdict block, and card grid into separate components for maintainability.
agents.py moderator parses LLM JSON; recommend defensive parse with a fallback verdict if the LLM returns malformed JSON (today it relies on Claude returning clean JSON every time).

success_rate.frontend: 100% on tested flows (landing, search, navigation, full SSE dashboard with verdict + all cards)

seed_data_creation: Generated AAPL and NVDA research reports via the live pipeline; persisted in MongoDB research_reports collection.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_alphalens_api.py -v. The SSE test reuses one AAPL pipeline run across multiple assertions via a module-scoped fixture (saves ~3-4 min). Pipeline runtime budget set at 180s to absorb LLM variance. Public URL comes from /app/frontend/.env (REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL). Bad-ticker test uses 'XYZQQ' which the providers fail on -> SSE emits an 'error' event correctly.
rca of the issue: Search-by-ticker miss: providers.py likely calls FMP's /search endpoint which performs fuzzy name match and weights ETFs that mention the ticker in their name. Mitigation: in search_ticker(), if the query is <=5 alphanumeric chars, first attempt get_profile(query.upper())/get_quote(query.upper()) and prepend the matching security to the results before returning.
